$comment: Test-only registry for the semantic-establishes Spectral fixture. Loaded via SPECTRAL_SEMANTIC_KINDS_REGISTRY env var.

kinds.name: OrphanedKind
kinds.$comment: Registered but never established in the fixture, to exercise the orphan-requires check.

kinds.name: Frob
kinds.$comment: Registered with an identifier but never established. Exercises the derived-requires orphan check on edge endpoints.

kinds.name: WidgetFrobLink
kinds.$comment: Edge whose endpoint Frob is unestablished — exercises derived-requires orphan.

kinds.name: WidgetMysteryLink
kinds.$comment: Edge whose endpoint semanticType resolves to no entity at all — exercises the unresolved-endpoint check.

kinds.name: ExternalThing
kinds.$comment: External entity (camunda/camunda#52320). Has no producer in the fixture spec; the cross-reference check must skip it.

kinds.name: WidgetExternalLink
kinds.$comment: Edge from Widget to ExternalThing — establishes correctly without needing an ExternalThing producer.

kinds.name: Bimodal
kinds.$comment: Registered entity with no producer in the fixture spec. Used to exercise the per-tuple `acceptsExternal: true` opt-out (camunda/camunda#52322): an edge endpoint with that flag must NOT trigger the derived-requires orphan check, even though Bimodal has no producer.

kinds.name: WidgetBimodalLink
kinds.$comment: Edge from Widget to Bimodal — uses identifiedBy.acceptsExternal: true on the Bimodal endpoint to declare that this site accepts either an in-API producer or an externally-minted ID.

kinds.name: BrokenProducedKind
kinds.$comment: Established by a fixture op whose `establishes` block is invalid (broken identifiedBy). The producer-existence gate must NOT count it as established, so consumers get the orphan error.

kinds.name: MalformedShapeKind
kinds.$comment: Class-scoped guard for the producer-existence gate's structural-shape validation (camunda/camunda#52322 review). Several fixture ops attempt to establish this kind via malformed `x-semantic-establishes` blocks (empty identifiedBy, missing identifiedBy, typo'd top-level key, non-object). The gate must NOT count any of them as a producer, so the downstream consumer still reports the orphan error.

kinds.name: LegacyBindKind
kinds.$comment: Class-scoped guard for the legacyBindKeys escape hatch (camunda/camunda#52322 review). Mirrors GlobalTaskListener: the published wire field is `id`, not `legacyBindKindId`, so a consumer binding via key `id` must lint clean even though `id` is not the camelCased identifier.

kinds.name: IsolatedKind
kinds.$comment: Class-scoped guard for the entity-tuple validation (camunda/camunda#52322 review). A producer fixture establishes this kind with a wrong `identifiedBy[].semanticType`; the producer must be rejected by the gate AND a downstream consumer must report orphan, proving the wrong semanticType did not silently satisfy producer-existence.
